explanation: This is the base command that matches the Sigma rule. It uses the exact syntax and arguments specified in the rule to kill the 'ccSvcHst.exe' process.
Inserts quotes around the image name, which is a common mutation technique. The command remains functionally identical but may evade detection if the rule does not account for quoted strings.
Adds an extra space at the end of the command line. This is a simple insertion that may bypass detection if the rule does not normalize whitespace.
Reorders the flags '/F' and '/IM'. Some programs accept reordered arguments, and this could evade detection if the rule does not account for argument order.
Alters the case of the filename. Windows is case-insensitive, so this should still work and may evade detection if the rule does not enforce case-insensitive matching.
Uses a relative path with '..\' to reference the same file. This is a form of path normalization and may evade detection if the rule does not normalize paths.
Replaces the filename with an environment variable. This is a form of recoding and may evade detection if the rule does not expand variables.
